tluaTableKeyResolution.tlua(7,7): error TLUA6133: 'unused' is declared but its value is never read.
tluaTableKeyResolution.tlua(12,23): error TLUA1117: An object literal cannot have multiple properties with the same name.
tluaTableKeyResolution.tlua(13,23): error TLUA1117: An object literal cannot have multiple properties with the same name.
tluaTableKeyResolution.tlua(14,27): error TLUA1117: An object literal cannot have multiple properties with the same name.


==== tluaTableKeyResolution.tlua (4 errors) ====
    // The name in `x = 1` is a property key, not a value reference: no `x`
    // needs to be in scope.
    local a = { x = 1 };
    local ax = a.x;
    
    // A key spelling a local's name is not a *use* of that local.
    local unused = 1;
          ~~~~~~
!!! error TLUA6133: 'unused' is declared but its value is never read.
    local u = { unused = 2 };
    local uu = u.unused;
    
    // Duplicate keys error across spellings (identifier vs bracket string).
    local d1 = { dup = 1, dup = 2 };
                          ~~~
!!! error TLUA1117: An object literal cannot have multiple properties with the same name.
    local d2 = { dup = 1, ["dup"] = 2 };
                          ~~~~~~~
!!! error TLUA1117: An object literal cannot have multiple properties with the same name.
    local d3 = { ["dup"] = 1, dup = 2 };
                              ~~~
!!! error TLUA1117: An object literal cannot have multiple properties with the same name.
    local ds = {d1, d2, d3};
    
    return { ax = ax, uu = uu, ds = ds };
